message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Air disinfection using Germicidal Ultraviolet light (GUV) has received increasing attention during the COVID-19 pandemic. GUV uses UVC lamps to inactivate microorganisms, but it also initiates photochemistry in air. However, GUV’s indoor-air-quality impact has not been investigated in detail. Here, we model the chemistry initiated by GUV at 254 (“GUV254”) or 222 nm (“GUV222”) in a typical indoor setting for different ventilation levels. Our analysis showed that GUV254, usually installed in the upper room, can significantly photolyze O
                  <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                  , generating OH radicals that oxidize indoor volatile organic compounds (VOCs) into more oxidized VOCs. Secondary organic aerosol (SOA) is also formed as a VOC-oxidation product. GUV254-induced SOA formation is of the order of 0.1-1 μg/m
                  <jats:sup>3</jats:sup>
                  for the cases studied here. GUV222 (described by some as harmless to humans and thus applicable for the whole room) with the same effective virus-removal rate makes a smaller indoor-air-quality impact at mid-to-high ventilation rates. This is mainly because of the lower UV irradiance needed and also less efficient OH-generating O
                  <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                  photolysis than GUV254. GUV222 has a higher impact than GUV254 under poor ventilation due to a small but significant photochemical production of O
                  <jats:sub>3</jats:sub>
                  at 222 nm, which does not occur with GUV254.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Synopsis</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Germicidal ultraviolet light initiates indoor oxidation chemistry, potentially forming indoor air pollutants. The amount is not negligible and depends on both the wavelength of light and the ventilation level.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Model Evaluation of Secondary Chemistry due to Disinfection of Indoor Air with Germicidal Ultraviolet Lamps
